intro: Ailurope (Ailuroschia galactites) is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the milkwort family (Polygalaceae), native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, Ailurope has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ailurope include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ailurope is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into cough syrups and expectorants.
